title: sideroxylon contrerasii
intro: Sideroxylon contrerasii, also known as the Mexican devil tree, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's leaves and bark have been used in herbal preparations to promote health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating coughs, and treating wounds. Sideroxylon contrerasii is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, showcasing its versatility in traditional medicine.
